reforms.description: The Employment Adjustment Grant scheme, effective from January 1975, provided financial support to temporarily laid-off workers, with public funds covering a significant portion of the allowance for large and small enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support to laid-off workers, the scheme helps maintain workforce stability and promotes occupational mobility, which can enhance overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been effective since January 1975.
reforms.importance_rationale: The scheme represents a significant intervention in the labor market, aimed at stabilizing employment during economic fluctuations, thus having a meaningful impact on human capital.
reforms.source_quote: Under this scheme, effective as from January 1975, of the allowance paid to temporarily laid-off workers, half was financed by public funds in the case of large enterprises and two-thirds in the case of small and medium-sized ones.
